name: Choucroute Garnie
recipeInstructions:
An Alsatian sauerkraut and meat dish
Polish sausage may be substituted for Italian sausage.
Cover bacon strips with boiling water; set aside. Cut pork into 1-inch chunks. Slice knackwurst and Italian sausages in half. Combine pork, knackwurst, and sausages and set aside. Drain sauerkraut, rinse in cold water, and squeeze dry. Drain bacon and pat dry. Chop bacon. Mix into sauerkraut and add crushed juniper berries and gin. Add half of the sauerkraut mixture to a crockpot, then add the mixed meats and remaining sauerkraut, sprinkling with pepper as you go. Pour wine over all and cover. Cook for 2 hours on High. Turn into warmed serving bowl. Sprinkle with parsley. Serve with boiled potatoes. Pass around mustard or horseradish, if desired.
Sylvia's comments: Cooking on HIGH in a crockpot resulted in better texture. A similar recipe with apples and brown sugar is preferred by some. This dish was served with applesauce to balance the bitterness, resulting in a delicious meal.
